channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: LC6 not working, 5th May
item.description:
LC6, first column on left down narrow cul-de-sac next to Trinity church, not working
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Trinity Place, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipped mattress, 5th May
item.description:
There is a fly-tipped mattress in the side road off Whetstone Road that leads to the car park running alongside St Nicholas Church. It was originally actually in Whetstone Road and has been moved to its current location. It has been there for several weeks.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Whetstone Road, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish piling up, rats infestation, building materials all dumped and left there for weeks, 5th May
item.description:
This pile of rubbish has been there for weeks. It attracts more fly tipping and causes rats infestation

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Herbert Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bollard on centre island not working, 5th May
item.description:
Bollard on centre island not working
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bourne Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bollard not illuminated, 5th May
item.description:
Crayford side bollard not illuminated
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bourne Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Disgusting overflowing bins, 5th May
item.description:
This is becoming ridiculous. The council won’t do anything about this, building management won’t do anything either. I have to live above the bin area and the smell is horrendous, and this is becoming a real public health issue.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Westgate Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pen tag on telecomms cabinet in Abbots Way Beckenham about 10m from the jct with Upper Elmers End Rd., 5th May
item.description:
Pen tag on telecomms cabinet in Abbots Way Beckenham about 10m from the jct with Upper Elmers End Rd. Streetview historic image. I forget the current colour of the cabinet, grey or green

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Abbots Way, Upper Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Report 2 - Brick bridge abutment on Upper Elmers End Rd, Beckenham on northern side near the jct with Adams Rd. Small pen or spray tag about 30cm x 50cm., 5th May
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Upper Elmers End Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The back door of 85 Priory Park Road that gives into Kingsley Road, 5th May
item.description:
The house is improperly occupied by a large number of people who have left some kitchen appliance and quite a few plastic chairs.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Priory Park Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: Racist Graffiti, 5th May
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 161, Chipperfield Road, St Paul's Cray, Greater London

item.title: Racist Graffiti on the bus shelter, 5th May
item.description:
Reform sayno catholics blax irish

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mickleham Road, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Several potholes along a cracked line on the road, 5th May
item.description:
There are numerous potholes and uneven road surface. There is a large crack down the road, a stretch of at least 5 meters with potholes large and deep
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A13, Grays
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

item.title: Trolley dumped by selfish moron, 5th May
item.description:
Doesn't belong here in an estate where houses cost more than £1m
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Morton Crescent, Southgate

item.title: Black pen tag on Fennies sign, South Eden Park Rd, Beckenham about 10 metres SW of the junction with Tiber Way., 5th May
item.description:

item.title: Graffitted bus stop, 5th May
item.description:
The graffiti on the bus time table: REFORM SAYNO CATHOLICS IRISH BLAX. Graffiti on the bus shelter: REFORM SAYNO COTHOLICS BLAX IRISH.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chipperfield Road, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside numbers 3 and 5, 5th May
item.description:
This pothole has been here for several months now - it is a sink hold rather than a normal pothole -the area affected is slowly sinking lower and now the pavement is collapsing too. It is a sink hole and I am worried could cause serious damage to surrounding houses or pedestrians or cars if left untreated. - I know it has been reported several times including 9370843 and has been ignored but it is getting deeper and bigger and urgently needs investigation - is it a drain issue maybe or something else that can sink. Compare this photo to the last report - it is deeper and larger and extending further into the road. Your response has been to say no action will be taken. If it causes more issues and subsidence we will be suoi h you for damage to our cars Josie’s and person
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 9, Downside Crescent, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Posts from new trees uprooted, 5th May
item.description:
The support posts from two trees which were planted last year have been uprooted for a couple of months. The trees themselves are still standing, but the posts present a hazard to the children who play on the verge.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kingsley Wood Drive, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Squatting in broken garage, 5th May
item.description:
One woman and groups of men squatting in a broken garage, antisocial behaviour, serious fire hazard, drugs and alcohol

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Church Walk, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
